patch.value: Current Pi wrapper mapping omits newer create/update metadata fields that are already canonical in PRD.md README.md and AGENTS.md. This task restores parity so Pi-driven maintainer runs can express full item intent without falling back to raw CLI invocation.
Docs-first align the Pi extension wrapper with the CLI's canonical create/update metadata surface so Pi calls can pass every current maintainer-required field deterministically.

Pi wrapper create/update mappings and schema expose the current canonical metadata fields required by the CLI docs, docs remain authoritative, and sandbox-safe wrapper/unit/release-contract coverage stays at 100 percent.
Gap confirmed by comparing .pi/extensions/pm-cli/index.ts against current create/update field contracts in PRD.md README.md and AGENTS.md.
Keep Pi wrapper action parity aligned with current CLI create/update contracts
Pi-based agents can use pm without losing required metadata
Removes docs-to-wrapper drift in release-readiness workflows
Pi wrapper forwards full create/update metadata deterministically
AGENTS.md now requires all fields/options on tracked items but the wrapper currently omits several canonical fields
